meta.title: International Invoices: How to Bill Overseas Clients Legally
meta.description: Learn how to format international invoices for cross-border services and goods. Covers currency conversion, VAT, and international wire transfer details.

blocks.title: How to Write an International Invoice
blocks.subtitle: Billing overseas clients requires more than just changing the currency symbol. Learn how to format your international invoices to ensure compliance and prompt payment.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.message: This guide provides general educational information regarding international billing and tax compliance. It does not constitute formal legal or tax advice. Cross-border tax treaties are complex. Always consult an international tax attorney.

blocks.heading: The Challenge of Cross-Border Billing
blocks.text: When you invoice a client in your own country, both parties share the same currency, tax laws, and banking systems. When you bill internationally, you introduce currency fluctuations, complex banking codes (SWIFT/IBAN), and foreign tax liabilities (like VAT or Withholding Tax). An improperly formatted international invoice will result in delayed wire transfers or, worse, double taxation.

summaryItems: State exactly which currency the invoice is in (e.g., 'USD' instead of just '$', as '$' could mean Canadian or Australian Dollars).
You must include your bank's SWIFT/BIC code and your IBAN for international wire transfers.
Check if 'Reverse Charge' VAT rules apply to your B2B service exports.
Include both countries in the billing and sender addresses.

steps.title: Establish the Currency
steps.description: Agree on the currency beforehand. If you bill in a foreign currency, you accept the exchange rate risk. If you bill in your home currency, the client bears the risk.

steps.title: Add International Banking Details
steps.description: A standard routing number is useless internationally. Provide your SWIFT/BIC code, IBAN (or account number), and the exact physical address of your bank's branch.

steps.title: Handle Cross-Border Taxes
steps.description: If you are exporting services (e.g., a US freelancer designing a website for a UK company), you generally do not charge US Sales Tax or UK VAT. Add a note: 'Export of Services - Subject to Reverse Charge' if applicable.

blocks.tip: International wire transfers often incur hidden intermediary bank fees (ranging from $15 to $50). Add a note to your invoice stating: 'Client is responsible for all international wire transfer fees. The final amount received must match the invoice total.'

questions.question: What is the difference between an International Invoice and a Commercial Invoice?
questions.description: A Commercial Invoice is strictly for shipping physical goods through customs. An 'International Invoice' is a broader term that also covers billing for digital services (like consulting) across borders, which does not require customs clearance.

blocks.heading: Ready to bill globally?
blocks.subtext: Our free invoice generator supports over 150 global currencies and custom tax labels.
